63ade128794bda10f6dedd79.question: Destinations in this region include the Isle of Slingers, Weatherbury Farm, Stancy Castle, and a famous church in Kingsbere. Newson landed in this region’s town of Havenpool upon returning from Newfoundland, and its town of Overcombe is mentioned in the historical novel The Trumpet-Major, which sees Anne Garland courted by three suitors, including the titular musician, John Loveday, of Budmouth. Budmouth is also home to horseraces frequented by Frank Troy, and Thomasin and Wildeve attempted to get married in this region’s town of Anglebury. Captain Thomas Hardy, who served as a commander under Lord Nelson at Trafalgar lived in Po’sham in this county, and Shaston is home to a school run by Mr. Philloston, the husband of Sue Bridehead. Tess Durbeyfield was executed in this county’s former seat of Wintoncester, and the town of Mellstock is where most of Under the Greenwood Tree is set. For 10 points, what is this semi-fictional county of southwestern England home to towns such as Casterbridge and Christminster that served as the setting for all of Thomas Hardy’s major novels?

63ade128794bda10f6dedda1.question: What name is shared by a geologic province and namesake town in Labrador, a town and county in Iran famous for its namesake rugs as well as the Narej Fortress, and an Arab-Israeli town mentioned in the New Testament as being the site of the miraculous revival of the son of a widow depicted in art by Lucas Cranach?
